data.text/markdown:
The compliance engine, one stage at a time

Building the secure cloud environment and proving it's compliant are the same action. This notebook runs the real engine end-to-end and shows what each step does with its actual input and output. It's a mock run — fixture data, Terraform in preview only, nothing deployed — so every artifact is stamped NON-EVIDENTIARY. A contract becomes a signed build Order, a Factory executes it and gathers evidence, machines run automated checks, and a human attests the result — with two safety gates that refuse to let the system fool itself.

data.text/html: <marimo-mermaid data-diagram='"\n    flowchart LR\n        C[\"Contract\\n(NV012)\"] --\u003e OB[\"Obligations\"]\n        OB --\u003e RC[\"Required\\ncontrols\"]\n        RC --\u003e COP[\"COP\\nAI drafts · human attests\"]\n        COP --\u003e G1{{\"GATE 1\\nplanning coverage\"}}\n        G1 --\u003e ORD[\"Signed\\nOrder\"]\n        ORD --\u003e FAC[\"Factory\\nplan · evidence · oracles\"]\n        FAC --\u003e G2{{\"GATE 2\\nhuman attests MET\"}}\n        G2 --\u003e AUD[\"Audit + SPRS\"]\n        AUD --\u003e BOM[\"BOM + SSP\\n(the proof)\"]\n    "'></marimo-mermaid>

Term Plain meaning
CMMC / NIST 800-171 The ~110 security rules a DoD contractor must follow to handle sensitive info (CUI).
Control One security rule (e.g. "require multi-factor login").
COP Contract Obligation Profile — the machine-readable list of what this contract requires.
Obligation One requirement pulled from the contract; resolves to a set of controls.
Order The signed, hash-referenced build order the Factory executes.
Module A piece of cloud setup (a KMS keyring, an IAM group) that satisfies one or more controls.
Evidence An artifact that addresses a control (a config export, a policy check). Never MET on its own.
Oracle An automated check: evidence vs a machine criterion → passed / failed / cantTell.
Attestation A human's recorded MET/NOT-MET judgment. Only this makes a control officially MET.
Gate 1 / Gate 2 Planning-coverage gate (before build) / fulfillment gate (human attests, at the end).
BOM / SSP Bill of Materials (machine proof) / System Security Plan (human doc). Content-addressed.
NON-EVIDENTIARY Stamp meaning "mock/practice artifact — not a real government submission."

• all-covered — everything the contract requires is covered and signed off → full score.
  • gap — require a control nothing implements → Gate 1 refuses, nothing is built.
  • contradiction — a human signs MET over a failed machine check → the audit flags it.
Change the dropdown and every cell below re-runs on the real engine.

<marimo-callout-output data-html='"\u003cspan class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"paragraph\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eScenario \u003ccode\u003eall-covered\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e — built a fresh knowledge graph from the committed catalog, structural model, and the NV012 COP (\u003ccode\u003efixtures/nv012/cop_draft.ttl\u003c/code\u003e). \u003cstrong\u003e9 obligations\u003c/strong\u003e loaded.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e"' data-kind='"neutral"'></marimo-callout-output>

data.text/markdown: Stage 1 · The contract → obligations

data.text/html:
Each obligation is one requirement pulled from the contract. The real resolver (rule_library.resolve) turns each into the controls it demands. A CUI/ITAR-marked deliverable trips a spillover guard rather than resolving silently.

data.text/markdown: Stage 2 · Obligations → the required-control set

data.text/html: compiler.resolve_required_controls unions every obligation's controls into the set this environment is responsible for — the scope the whole run is graded on.Controls: AC.L2-3.1.1, AC.L2-3.1.2, AC.L2-3.1.3, AC.L2-3.1.5, AU.L2-3.3.1, AU.L2-3.3.2, AU.L2-3.3.5, CM.L2-3.4.1, CM.L2-3.4.2, CM.L2-3.4.6, CM.L2-3.4.7, IA.L2-3.5.2, IA.L2-3.5.3, IA.L2-3.5.4, PE.L2-3.10.1, PE.L2-3.10.2, SC.L2-3.13.1, SC.L2-3.13.10, SC.L2-3.13.11, SC.L2-3.13.16, SI.L2-3.14.3, SI.L2-3.14.6No policy markers — this contract's obligations resolve entirely to controls, with no ITAR / US-person / residency overlay to carry alongside them.

data.text/markdown:
Stage 3 · The COP attestation — AI drafts, a human attests

The founding rule of the whole system: a machine may draft and gather, but only a human validation carries legal weight. Here the Compliance Officer attests the Contract Obligation Profile.

Stage 4 · GATE 1 — planning coverage (the first beat)

Before anything is built, the system refuses to proceed unless the plan is complete in both directions: every required control has a module claiming it (forward), every module traces back to a required control (backward), and no claim lacks a testable method. A gap ⇒ the Order is refused and the missing control is named.
Stage 5 · The signed Order

data.text/markdown:
Stage 6 · The Factory — build it and gather the proof

The Factory executes the Order: fetch the modules by hash, terraform plan the environment, run a policy check before any apply, apply (mock), gather evidence, and run the machine oracles. Oracles evaluate evidence against a criterion — they never attest.

data.text/markdown:
Stage 7 · GATE 2 — the human attests MET

A machine can gather evidence and run checks, but it can never declare a control satisfied. Only the Affirming Official does — carrying the legal accountability.

data.text/html: <marimo-callout-output data-html='"\u003cspan class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"paragraph\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e22 controls attested MET\u003c/strong\u003e by the NV012 Affirming Official — each with a written adequacy + sufficiency rationale. Machine-checked controls carry their real oracle outcome, so a MET-over-a-failed-check becomes a visible contradiction (Stage 8).\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e"' data-kind='"success"'></marimo-callout-output>

# SSP-NV012 — NV012 System Security Plan + Traceability Matrix (Document 2)

> ⚠ **NON-EVIDENTIARY — fixture-derived / auto-attested.**
> Evidentiary status present: mock. This is a demonstration artifact, **not a submittable SSP**.

| --- | --- |
| Document ID | SSP-NV012 |
| System | NV012 Tier 1 IL4 CUI enclave |
| CUI boundary | Google Workspace Enterprise Plus + GCP Assured Workloads (IL4) |

| Evidentiary status | NON-EVIDENTIARY (mock) |
| Compiler | documents/ssp.py |

## 2. Framework applicability

Scope: NIST SP 800-171 Rev. 2 / CMMC Level 2 (110 controls). Status is the
recorded human attestation (EARL outcome via `STATUS_LABEL`); evidence
*addresses* controls but never *attests* them. The machine-checkable subset
is verified by oracles; the remainder is human-attested from documentary
evidence.

## 3. Verification Cross-Reference Matrix (VCRM) — Document 2

One row per control (all 110). Status is the attestation outcome; a control
with no attestation is PLANNED (a gap).

| Control | Implementation | Responsible party | Evidence location | Evidence hash | Status | Gap notes | POA&M ref |

data.text/markdown: The substrate — one queryable knowledge graph

data.text/html:
Every stage wrote its own named graph into one RDF dataset — controls, the Order, evidence, attestations, the audit — all separated but queryable together. This is what makes the whole chain re-executable and tamper-evident.

The engine \u003cem\u003ecomputes\u003c/em\u003e the score; a human still submits it.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"paragraph\"\u003eNone of this is hidden — the banner, the \u003ccode\u003emock\u003c/code\u003e stamp, and the machine-vs-human split are\nall there to keep the demo honest. The \u003cem\u003emechanism\u003c/em\u003e is fully wired end to end.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e"' data-kind='"neutral"'></marimo-callout-output>
